title: Here, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu teaches the Dhamma to others extensively as he has heard and learned. He spends the whole day explaining the Dhamma to…
content:
Here, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu teaches the Dhamma to others extensively as he has heard and learned. He spends the whole day explaining the Dhamma to others, neglecting the peaceful life of seclusion, not striving to focus on internal serenity of mind. This, bhikkhus, is called a bhikkhu who is much in recitation but does not live in accordance with the Dhamma.
Furthermore, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu recites the Dhamma extensively as he has heard and learned. He spends the whole day reciting, neglecting the peaceful life of seclusion, not striving to focus on internal serenity of mind. This, bhikkhus, is called a bhikkhu who is much in recitation but does not live in accordance with the Dhamma.

title: Furthermore, bhikkhus, with sensual desire as cause, with sensual desire as condition, with sensual desire as reason, with sensual desire itself as cause, they take…
content: Furthermore, bhikkhus, with sensual desire as cause, with sensual desire as condition, with sensual desire as reason, with sensual desire itself as cause, they take up spears and shields, they don bows and arrows, they attack fortified citadels newly plastered with lime, and arrows fly, and swords flash. They are pierced by arrows, slashed by swords, drenched with boiling excrement, crushed with stones, and their heads are cut off. There they meet death or deadly pain. Bhikkhus, such is the danger of sensual desires, a mass of suffering visible here and now, with sensual desire as cause, with sensual desire as condition, with sensual desire as source, with sensual desire as reason.

title: What is one? All beings subsist on food. What are two? Name and form. What are three? Three kinds of feeling. What are four? Four…
content:
What is one? All beings subsist on food.
What are two? Name and form.
What are three? Three kinds of feeling.
What are four? Four noble truths.
What are five? Five aggregates subject to clinging.
What are six? Six internal sense spheres.
What are seven? Seven factors of enlightenment.
What are eight? The noble eightfold path.
What are nine? Nine abodes of beings.
What are ten? One endowed with ten qualities is called an Arahant.

title: Householder, just as a house with a poorly thatched roof — the ridgepole is not protected, the rafters are not protected, the walls are not…
content: Householder, just as a house with a poorly thatched roof — the ridgepole is not protected, the rafters are not protected, the walls are not protected; the ridgepole becomes saturated and leaks, the rafters become saturated and leak, the walls become saturated and leak; the ridgepole decays, the rafters decay, the walls decay. So too, householder, when the mind is not protected, bodily action is not protected, verbal action... mental action... then bodily action becomes saturated with lust and leaks; verbal action becomes saturated with lust and leaks; mental action becomes saturated with lust and leaks. For one whose bodily action is saturated with lust and leaks, whose verbal action... mental action... then bodily action decays, verbal action decays, mental action decays. For one whose bodily action decays, verbal action decays, mental action decays, death is not good, the end is not good.

title: Elder Subhūti: My hut is well thatched, peaceful, free from wind. O deva of rain, rain if you wish! My mind is well concentrated, liberated,…
content:
Elder Subhūti:

My hut is well thatched, peaceful, free from wind.
O deva of rain, rain if you wish!
My mind is well concentrated, liberated, dwelling ardently.
O deva of rain, rain if you wish!

title: Furthermore, friends, when a bhikkhu, in the presence or absence of his fellow celibates, maintains bodily acts of loving-kindness, this is a quality of concord,…
content: Furthermore, friends, when a bhikkhu, in the presence or absence of his fellow celibates, maintains bodily acts of loving-kindness, this is a quality of concord, creating loving-kindness, creating respect, leading to unity, non-disputing, harmony, unanimity, unity of mind.

title: Possessing six qualities, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu dwells happily in this very life, without trouble, without distress, without fever; and after death, with the breakup of…
content: Possessing six qualities, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu dwells happily in this very life, without trouble, without distress, without fever; and after death, with the breakup of the body, a good destination is to be expected. With thoughts of renunciation, thoughts of non-ill-will, thoughts of non-harming, perceptions of renunciation, perceptions of non-ill-will, perceptions of non-harming. Possessing these six qualities, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu dwells happily in this very life, without trouble, without distress, without fever; and after death, with the breakup of the body, a good destination is to be expected.

title: And, bhikkhus, what is a bhikkhu who dwells alone? Here, bhikkhus, for a bhikkhu, conceit has been abandoned, cut off at the root, made like…
content:
And, bhikkhus, what is a bhikkhu who dwells alone? Here, bhikkhus, for a bhikkhu, conceit has been abandoned, cut off at the root, made like a palm stump, made non-existent, made unable to arise in the future. Thus, bhikkhus, is a bhikkhu who dwells alone. A bhikkhu who has removed personal truth, bhikkhus, who has completely abandoned all searches, or whose bodily activities are calmed — he is called one who walks alone.

The search for sensual pleasures and existence,
And the search for the holy life,
Clinging to this truth,
The accumulation of views.

Who does not delight in all greed,
Liberated through the destruction of craving,
Has abandoned the searches,
Uprooted the grounds of views.

That bhikkhu is peaceful,
Truly mindful, calm,
Not defeated by anyone,
With conceit fully seen,
He is called by name,
The Enlightened One who dwells alone.

title: Bhikkhus, because of not abandoning three things, one cannot abandon forgetfulness, cannot abandon lack of clear comprehension, cannot abandon an agitated mind. What three? Not…
content: Bhikkhus, because of not abandoning three things, one cannot abandon forgetfulness, cannot abandon lack of clear comprehension, cannot abandon an agitated mind. What three? Not abandoning the desire not to visit the noble ones, not abandoning the desire not to listen to the noble Dhamma, not abandoning fault-finding. Because of not abandoning these three things, bhikkhus, one cannot abandon forgetfulness, cannot abandon lack of clear comprehension, cannot abandon an agitated mind.

title: Just as, bhikkhus, a house with a roof or a roofed veranda, with windows on the north, south, or east. When the sun rises, the…
content:
Just as, bhikkhus, a house with a roof or a roofed veranda, with windows on the north, south, or east. When the sun rises, the light enters through the window — where does it fall?" — "Venerable Sir, it falls on the western wall." — "Bhikkhus, if there is no western wall, where does it fall?" — "Venerable Sir, it falls on the ground." — "Bhikkhus, if there is no ground, where does it fall?" — "Venerable Sir, it falls on the water." — "Bhikkhus, if there is no water, where does it fall?" — "Venerable Sir, it falls nowhere."

So too, bhikkhus, if there is no lust, no delight, no craving for consciousness as nutriment, consciousness does not become established. Where consciousness does not become established, there is no descent of name-and-form. Where there is no descent of name-and-form, there is no growth of formations ... no future renewed existence ... no future birth, aging, death ... I say that there is no sorrow, no suffering, no despair.
